The production FutarchyFactory at 0xa6cB18FCDC17a2B44E5cAd2d80a6D5942d30a345 is NOT implemented here — it appears only as an interface (src/interfaces/IFutarchyFactory.sol) plus scripts/tests that call the already-deployed Gnosis instance via a FUTARCHY_FACTORY env var; no Reality.eth or seer-pm code exists in src/ or lib/. contracts.reuse_assessment: Reusable for a Sepolia fire-the-CEO product: (1) src/core/GnosisCTFAdapter.sol — directly implements the primitive the product needs: split a collateral/value-proxy ERC20 on a binary condition ("CEO X leaves by T") into tradable SYMBOL-Y / SYMBOL-N ERC20s and redeem after resolution; it is permissionless, small (187 lines), unit-tested, and chain-agnostic (constructor takes CTF + Wrapped1155Factory addresses, so it deploys to Sepolia unchanged once you deploy ConditionalTokens and Wrapped1155Factory there from the lib/ submodule sources). (2) The interfaces (src/interfaces/IConditionalTokens.sol, IWrapped1155Factory.sol, IFutarchyFactory/IFutarchyProposal/IFutarchyRouter) as ABI references if we instead port the production seer-pm-style factory. (3) src/UniswapV3PassthroughRouter.sol + script/deploy/DeployUniswapV3PassthroughRouter.s.sol — dependency-free, deployable to Sepolia as-is if we use Uniswap v3 pools for the conditional markets. (4) src/combinatorial/EventContract.sol as a minimal trusted-oracle binary condition registry pattern (but simpler to just use CTF's prepareCondition/reportPayouts directly). NOT reusable: the production FutarchyFactory/Proposal/Router stack (not in this repo — must come from the seer-pm-style repo or be purpose-built; Reality.eth is Gnosis/mainnet-oriented and overkill for a Sepolia demo where a simple owned oracle suffices); FutarchyPoolManager + BalancerPoolWrapper (Balancer integration is fictional/broken — uninitialized poolId, nonexistent Vault.createPool signature); src/core/FutarchyOracle/Governor/ProposerGuard and src/FAO/* (DAO-execution and proposal-auction machinery irrelevant to a ranking product, plus Chainlink VRF dependency); src/liquidity/ and script/proposal/ (hardwired to SushiSwap + WXDAI + the Gnosis factory); src/combinatorial/ as a whole (untested, unaudited, ERC1155 positions would still need ERC20 wrapping for AMM pools — the full Hanson combinatorial machinery is unnecessary for pairwise fire/keep markets). Net recommendation this slice supports: purpose-built minimal path = deploy CTF + Wrapped1155Factory + GnosisCTFAdapter to Sepolia, one condition per (company, CEO, horizon), split a mock company-value token, two pools per company (value|fired vs value|stays) on Uniswap v3 or a trivial CPMM, fire-premium ranking computed off-chain from pool prices — rather than resurrecting this repo's pool-manager stack.
risks: BalancerPoolWrapper is silently broken (uninitialized poolId in addLiquidity at src/pools/BalancerPoolWrapper.sol:~78, fictional IBalancerVault.createPool signature): anything copied from the FutarchyPoolManager path will fail on-chain against real Balancer even though it compiles and its mock-based tests pass

interface+sdk.reuse_assessment: HIGHLY REUSABLE for ceo.futarchy.fi on Sepolia: (1) The market-creation pipeline futarchy-sdk/refactorCreateProposal — it is config-driven per chain (chains.config.json) and is exactly what created the mainnet SBUX/TSLA CEO markets on Uniswap V3; adding Sepolia = one new chain block (chainId 11155111, Sepolia RPC, canonical Uniswap V3 Sepolia NFPM/factory/router addresses) + a FutarchyFactory/adapter deployment on Sepolia (contracts repo's job) + mock ERC20 company/currency tokens. Its Ethereum/Uniswap code path is the natural template since Sepolia has no Algebra/Swapr. (2) The market-page rendering stack: MarketPageShowcase + TripleChart (lightweight-charts) + ShowcaseSwapComponent/ConfirmSwapModal's chain-1 Uniswap V3 path, plus the FutarchyRouter split/merge UX. (3) The fire-the-ceo page skeleton (banner + impact-ranked card grid) is literally the product's ranking page waiting for real data — swap mockCards for computed fire premiums. (4) Wallet scaffolding: add `sepolia` from wagmi/chains + a transport in src/providers/providers.jsx (two-line change conceptually). (5) Netlify static-export deployment recipe transfers directly to a new site at ceo.futarchy.fi. NOT REUSABLE / HEAVY LIFT: (a) The entire data layer assumes api.futarchy.fi Checkpoint registry+candles indexers which only index Gnosis/Ethereum — a Sepolia deployment needs either a new Checkpoint indexer instance or replacing subgraph reads with direct RPC reads (for a small fixed set of CEO markets, direct RPC + a static config is far simpler); price-history charts specifically die without an indexer. (b) The on-chain metadata registry (aggregator/organization/proposal-metadata factories) exists only on Gnosis; for a purpose-built product, skip it and hardcode a companies+markets config file (the repo already does this pattern in src/config/markets.js). (c) futarchy-complete-sdk needs surgery to leave Gnosis (~15 hardcoded `chain: gnosis` literals, dead CloudFront subgraph URLs) — prefer refactorCreateProposal over it for Sepolia writes. (d) The app overall is a sprawling legacy codebase (3600-line swap modal, ethers v5 + v6 + viem mix, dozens of dead docs/dirs); for a clean ceo.futarchy.fi, extracting the chart + swap + card components into a fresh minimal Next/Vite app is likely cheaper than adopting the whole repo. Product-model note: one FutarchyProposal already contains BOTH conditional legs (YES=CEO-leaves and NO=CEO-stays pools over the company token), so the 'pair of conditional markets' maps to ONE proposal per company, and the fire premium = YES-pool price minus NO-pool price — computable exactly like the mock cards' 'Predicted Impact'. Purpose-built minimal contracts are only needed if the futarchy contracts repo can't be deployed to Sepolia.
risks: No Sepolia support exists anywhere in this repo: wagmi chains are hardwired [mainnet, gnosis], SDK CHAIN_CONFIG has only 1/100(/137 in refactorCreateProposal), and the only testnet scaffold (Chiado PERMISSIONLESS_TESTNET_DEFAULTS in src/features/marketCreation/marketCreationWorkflow.js) has every contract address empty — there is no precedent of a working testnet deployment to copy

Hard dependency on api.futarchy.fi Checkpoint indexers for proposal metadata AND candle charts; no Sepolia indexer exists, so charts/history/ranking data need either new indexer infra or an on-chain-read redesign

futarchy-complete-sdk's own subgraph config still points at the dead CloudFront endpoint (d3ugkaojqkfud0.cloudfront.net) — its read tools are likely broken as-is and must be repointed to api.futarchy.fi before reuse

old-ceo-markets.summary: Fire-the-CEO artifacts DO exist in futarchy-fi, in three layers. (1) Two real on-chain markets on Ethereum mainnet, created by the verified futarchy-fi FutarchyFactory (0xf9369c0F7a84CAC3b7Ef78c837cF7313309D3678, Seer-fork: Reality.eth v3 + Kleros arbitrator + Gnosis CTF): a public Tesla market "Will Tesla shareholders approve the 2025 CEO Performance Award ('Mega Package')..." (proposal 0x2A4b52B47625431Fdc6fE58CeD3086E76c1f6bbf, collateral TSLAON Ondo tokenized Tesla stock + USDS, created 2025-09-23, resolved) and a hidden/never-launched Starbucks fire-the-CEO test market "Will Starbucks CEO Brian Niccol be terminated or resign before 2026-01-01?" (proposal 0x1F54f0312E85c5AFACe2bDF15AA2514BeFDB844F, collateral SBUXON + USDS, created 2025-10-13, left private/pending_review/unresolved). (2) A live but orphaned mock-data marketing page at https://futarchy.fi/fire-the-ceo (interface repo, Apr 2025) with 6 hardcoded cards (Tim Cook +15.2%, Musk -8.5%, Nadella +3.1%, Pichai +1.8%, Zuckerberg +22.7%, Bezos -5.0%) plus an unshipped CEO photo roster (Jensen Huang, Brian Armstrong, Saylor, Ryan Cohen, Phong Le, Yan Li) in public/assets/ceos/. (3) An archived Apr-2025 backend POC (futarchy-services PR #2 "Fire the CEO") that just adds a firing_ceo boolean + POST /{event_id}/fire-ceo endpoint. Structurally the on-chain markets already implement Hanson's pair-of-conditional-markets (tokenized share price under YES vs NO via CTF split of equity+cash collateral); what was never built is the standing per-company pair on the fire/keep decision and the cross-company fire-premium ranking. hub/BACKLOG.md still lists "fire-the-CEO/Ondo" as an untracked revenue lead.

hanson-vision.summary: Hanson's fire-the-CEO vision is stable across 30 years of primary sources: for each public company, run a PAIR of conditional stock markets — company stock traded for cash where trades are "called off (i.e., made null and void)" if the CEO condition fails — one conditional on the CEO leaving by a stated horizon (quarter end in the canonical OldTek example; 1 year in the 1996 original), one conditional on staying. The decision signal is the price gap, judged over a pre-announced measurement window (his canonical rule: dump-side bid above keep-side ask for 90% of the last week of the quarter; his 2024+ refinement: a statistical model of prices confident the gap isn't chance). Non-negotiables he keeps repeating: real subsidies/subsidized market makers (he invented LMSR for exactly this thin-market problem), insiders allowed and encouraged to trade (CEO trades disclosed, outsiders anonymous), a pre-announced decision timeframe with the decision maker's info revealed to traders (to defeat decision-selection bias), and at minimum advisory use by boards that builds a track record. His criticism of Augur/Gnosis-era crypto attempts is that they built generic platforms with no specific decisions, no clients, no subsidies, and no decision linkage; he now points to MetaDAO and Futarchy.fi (this team's own stack) as the live trials of his "best idea".
key_facts: MARKET PAIR DEFINITION (canonical, 'Shall We Vote on Values, But Bet on Beliefs?', 2000/2007/2013, https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/futarchy.pdf, published version https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/futarchy2013.pdf): 'A financial house agreed to ensure that there are always dump-the-OldTek-CEO stock markets, trading OldTek stock conditional on dumping the current CEO at the end of the current quarter... these agreements to trade would be called off (i.e., made null and void) if the CEO were not dumped by the end of the current quarter.' Payoff numeraire = the company's stock itself vs cash; the conditional price IS the expected stock value under that regime.

TOKENIZED EQUIVALENT Hanson himself gives (footnote 6, same paper): 'Alternatively, the assets could be contingent instead of the trades. That is, assets of the form "Pays $1 if CEO dumped" could be traded for assets of the form "Pays one OldTek stock if CEO dumped".' This is exactly the Gnosis-CTF/futarchy-fi conditional-token construction — split collateral into IF-DUMPED/IF-KEPT legs, void (full-refund/merge) the leg whose condition fails.

DECISION RULE (same paper, verbatim): 'The OldTek corporate charter might say that if the dump-the-CEO price were clearly higher than the keep-the-CEO stock price, the board must dump him within the next quarter. (The price might be deemed "clearly higher" if the dump-the-CEO bid price were above the keep-the-CEO ask price for 90% of the last week of a quarter.)' — i.e., bid-vs-ask dominance over a sustained averaging window, not an instantaneous mid-price crossing.

DECISION RULE 2024 REFINEMENT (Futarchy Details, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/futarchy-details): 'Make a statistical model of the two prices over/near the decision period' and approve only if the model is 'confident enough' the period-end price difference isn't chance; plus 'extra strong liquidity subsidy just before the period' when the price difference is measured; agenda via auctioning regular proposal slots.

ADVISORY MODE PROTOCOL (Advisory Futarchy, 2025-05-11, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/advisory-futarchy): futarchy 'could advise whether to keep or dump the CEO'; Condition A: declare the decision timeframe in advance and use market price AVERAGES over a pre-set window ('48 and 24 hours seem sufficient'); Condition B: 'Allow and encourage key decision makers and advisors who have access to inside info relevant to the decision to trade in the markets'; subsidize the run-up period; decision makers start advisory and 'slowly put more weight on them as they proved themselves via concrete track records'.

ORIGINAL 1996 PROPOSAL (Markets for Dumping CEOs, 1996-04-26, https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/dumpceo.html, verbatim): 'I propose to create, for each stock, a separate market in that stock for trades which are "called-off" if the CEO does not step down in the next year... If that stock price is consistently and significantly higher than the ordinary stock price, that should be a clear market signal, from informed traders, for the CEO to step down. (If there is no price, because there is no trading, then there is no signal.)' Users: boards ('board members may more plausibly fear legal action for ignoring these market signals') and shame-publicity in the 'Just Vote No' campaign context where worst companies 'are then publicized' — the seed of the ranking/scoreboard idea. Note: 1996 compares dump-conditional vs ORDINARY spot price with a 1-year horizon; from 2000 on he uses the explicit dump/keep pair at quarter horizon.

FORMAL MATH (Decision Market Math, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/decision-market-mathhtml): with A = 'CEO stays through quarter end', split $1 into '$1 if A' + '$1 if not A'; x1=E[x|A] from trading '$x if A' against '$1 if A', x2=E[x|not A] likewise; CEO value-added = x1-x2.

SUBSIDIES ARE MANDATORY: 1999 IEEE Intelligent Systems 'Decision Markets' (https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/decisionmarkets.pdf): 'a subsidized market can function over any time period with only one trader'; subsidy 'in effect creates an information prize'; Futarchy Details: 'Better to just subsidize these markets... net subsidies on average roughly a third of the value that proposals add.' The subsidized automated market maker is his own invention — LMSR (Logarithmic Market Scoring Rules, https://mason.gmu.edu/~rhanson/mktscore.pdf; also 'Combinatorial Information Market Design' 2003).

MANIPULATION RESISTANCE (futarchy paper + experiments): 'on average interested parties cannot bias the price; they can at worst only add noise'; manipulators are just noise traders that increase returns to informed trading — cites Hanson & Oprea 'Manipulators increase information market accuracy' and Hanson/Oprea/Porter JEBO 2006, Camerer 1998. Insider-trading design: guarantee ANONYMITY for non-insiders (the 'Disgruntled Jones' junior employee can bet against his CEO without retribution); require the CEO's (and insiders') trades to be identified and his compensation public.

DECISION SELECTION BIAS — the conditional!=causal trap (Decision Selection Bias, 2024-12-28, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/decision-selection-bias; also 'Correlation Is Not Causation' section of the futarchy paper): if the board knows more than traders, prices conditional on dump/keep are biased. Fixes he specifies: (1) reveal decision-maker info to speculators or let those holding it trade; (2) 'clearly inform speculators that a decision is about to be made' in a short pre-announced window; (3) commit to randomization — e.g., act on prices conditional on dice='snake eyes'; (4) 'randomly reject say 5% of the changes that it would otherwise have accepted' so the not-adopt condition stays measurable. A real product should implement the random-veto and announced-decision-window mechanics.

WHO USES IT (Hanania interview, 2021-09-15, https://www.richardhanania.com/p/futarchy-robin-hanson-on-how-prediction): boards are the primary audience ('look at that price difference and say, Should we keep him, or should we dump him?'); mechanism extends to ranking alternative CEO candidates and cascading down to CFO/regional managers/ad agencies. Regulatory limit: conditional stock markets are a stock derivative needing SEC/CFTC permission; the deeper barrier is organizational — the markets are 'a very knowledgeable autist in the C suite' that 'will often say things that people do not want to hear'.

WHY PAST ATTEMPTS FAILED, PER HANSON (Prediction Markets Update, 2017-09-19, https://www.overcomingbias.com/2017/09/prediction-markets-update.html + Hanania interview): Augur/Gnosis were 'general platforms, expecting others to fill in the specific betting topics and to do the marketing'; crypto teams were 'software people and algorithm people' not 'business people who work with particular clients'; corporate-adjacent products 'keep the appearance of markets but undercut their main incentive benefits' by excluding insider-relevant topics; combinatorial/subsidized mechanisms remain an untried 'overhang'. Actual 2016 Gnosis futarchy experiments (Ethereum Foundation grant to Liston/Koppelmann, incl. a hire/fire-CEO test) never became a product (https://medium.com/@consensus/an-introduction-to-cryptoeconomics-and-futarchy-experiments-on-gnosis-df85220f840a, https://www.charterless.com/p/a-3b-bet-on-predicting-the-future); GnosisDAO futarchy (2020) stayed advisory-internal (https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2020/11/23/new-gnosisdao-bets-on-futarchy-a-prediction-market-governance-model).

CURRENT STATE PER HANSON (My Best Idea: Decision Markets, 2026-04-25, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/my-best-idea-decision-markets): he names the live trials as MetaDAO and Futarchy.fi (i.e., this team's own stack); 'Most all innovations combine simple elegant ideas with messy details' needing 'concrete trials and experiments'. He became a paid MetaDAO adviser Feb 2025 — hired via a futarchy market on his own hiring (https://www.coindesk.com/business/2025/02/13/metadao-hires-futarchy-s-creator-robin-hanson-as-advisor). Power Futarchy (2026-04-21, https://www.overcomingbias.com/p/power-futarchy): profit-futarchy stalls because 'top people, like the CEO, often do not see their personal success as maxed by firm success' — CEOs block it; implication: a fire-the-CEO product should serve outside audiences (boards under pressure, activists, media), not wait for CEO consent.

RANKING CAVEAT (epistemic flag): Hanson explicitly proposed a market 'for each stock' (1996) and endorsed publicizing worst-governed companies (Just Vote No context), and in interviews says media/boards should read the price gaps; but no verbatim Hanson quote proposing a single 'rank all public companies by fire premium' scoreboard was found in this sweep. The scoreboard is a faithful extrapolation (he framed the signal as comparable across firms and complementary to published shame-rankings), not a documented spec item.
hanson-vision.reuse_assessment: For Hanson to say 'this is my vision, done right', a ceo.futarchy.fi product must include: (1) PAIRED conditional markets per company — stock-value-if-CEO-out-by-T vs stock-value-if-CEO-in-at-T — implemented either as called-off trades or, per his own footnote, as conditional tokens ('pays $1 if dumped' vs 'pays one share if dumped'), with the failing condition's leg fully voided/refunded, never settled; the futarchy-fi/Gnosis-CTF split-merge-redeem machinery is a direct match here and is reusable as-is. (2) A concrete outcome: (proxy for) stock price at a fixed date T+delta after the horizon, with the CEO-departure condition resolved by an objective oracle definition (departure announced/effective by T — must be pinned precisely, since resignations vs firings blur it). (3) A published, pre-announced measurement window and decision rule — his canonical spec is 'dump-side bid > keep-side ask for 90% of the last week of the quarter', modernized to a time-averaged gap ('fire premium') with statistical confidence, not spot mid-prices; the ranking page should therefore rank by time-averaged premium over the window, and show 'no signal' when there's no liquidity ('if there is no price... there is no signal'). (4) Subsidized liquidity via an automated market maker — he invented LMSR for exactly this; a fixed per-market subsidy with extra liquidity near the measurement window is required, and pure orderbook/no-subsidy markets would earn his 2017 criticism; futarchy-fi's AMM approach qualifies if a sponsor funds the LP loss as an explicit information subsidy. (5) Insider participation design: anonymous trading for outsiders, disclosed trading for the CEO/insiders — approximable on-chain (pseudonymous by default; a voluntary 'disclosed insider' flag), and decision-selection-bias mitigations (announced decision windows; ideally a random-veto commitment if any decision linkage exists). (6) An audience strategy aimed at boards, activist investors, and media via a public cross-company premium ranking — this is the part NO past attempt delivered and where the product differentiates. What is NOT reusable/achievable on Sepolia: real-money incentives (testnet play money gives no information-revelation incentive, so Hanson would call it a demo of the mechanism, not the mechanism — frame it explicitly as a trial, which he endorses: 'concrete trials and experiments'); board commitments (no real board will bind to it — run it in his 'advisory futarchy' mode and build a track record); and real stock as numeraire (SEC derivative territory — must use a synthetic index of the actual share price via oracle, which changes the hedging economics and should be documented as a deviation). The 1996 dump-vs-spot single-market design is superseded — build the explicit pair, per his 2000+ writings.
risks: Decision selection bias / conditional-vs-causal gap: with purely advisory markets and better-informed boards, the fire premium can be biased (Hanson's own 2024-12-28 post and the dynomight critique he engaged); without announced decision windows or randomization the ranking measures correlation (bad-shape companies both fire CEOs and decline), and critics will attack the product on exactly this.

Thin/no liquidity: Hanson is explicit that no trading = no signal and that unsubsidized conditional markets stay empty; a 500-company ranking with unfunded LMSR subsidies produces noise premia that misrank companies and discredit the scoreboard.

Play-money testnet: no real stakes means no incentive to reveal information — Hanson's core mechanism is absent; over-claiming 'Hanson's vision, done' for a Sepolia demo invites his own 2017-style criticism of appearance-only markets.

Condition definition/oracle risk: 'CEO leaves by T' is endogenous and gameable (planned retirements, health exits, title shuffles to executive chairman); resolution rules must pre-specify what counts or premia become uninterpretable.

Legal: for the real-money version this is a security derivative (Hanson: 'You need some sort of permission there' re SEC/CFTC); even testnet marketing that implies tradable exposure to named public companies' stock needs care.

Manipulation defense assumes many competing traders and public knowledge of manipulation incentives; in tiny markets a motivated party (e.g., an activist fund talking its book, or the CEO's allies) can hold a premium at a chosen level for the whole averaging window.

Reputational: naming real CEOs on a public 'should be fired' ranking with thin play-money prices risks defamation-adjacent blowback and gives futarchy skeptics an easy target; Hanson's Power Futarchy post predicts CEOs will actively fight it rather than ignore it.

sepolia-inventory.reuse_assessment: Highly reusable: the whole market-plumbing layer for a pair-of-conditional-markets product already exists on Sepolia and is operated by our key. The FAO 'shared' set (WETH 0xfFf9..., CTF 0x8bdC..., Wrapped1155Factory 0xD194..., UniV3 factory/NPM/QuoterV2/SwapRouter02) is exactly the primitive stack for fire-the-CEO: splitCollateral on a binary condition ('CEO X departs by T'), wrap outcome ERC1155s to ERC20 via Wrapped1155Factory, and spin up two UniV3 pools (value-if-fired vs value-if-stays); fire premium = TWAP/spot difference between the pools, readable via QuoterV2 or pool.observe(). The FAO repo (/home/kelvin/repos/futarchy-fi/FAO) also provides reusable deploy scripts, a deployments.json manifest convention consumed by CI + static site, a CDN-ethers static-frontend pattern (site-testnet/), fork-test mode, and bytecode-coupling checks. Reality.eth v3 (0xaf33...49CA) is live on Sepolia and is the natural resolution oracle for the real-world 'CEO left by T' fact — note FAO deliberately dropped Reality.eth in favor of a FutarchyTwapResolver, which resolves by comparing conditional TWAPs and is NOT suitable for resolving a real-world CEO-departure event. NOT reusable / new work needed: FAO's governance-specific machinery (FAOSale, FAOToken, FutarchyArbitration, registry/orchestrator/token_arb_deployer, 2-phase instance creation) solves a different problem (FAO self-governance) and adds complexity a per-company market-pair product doesn't need. Biggest gap: public companies have no on-chain value token — we must define the traded asset per company (e.g. an oracle/Reality.eth-settled synthetic 'company value at horizon' token, or virtual-collateral prediction-market prices as value proxies); nothing in the existing stack provides this. Ranking/'fire premium' leaderboard is pure frontend+indexer work with no existing component. Recommendation: purpose-built minimal contracts (condition + synthetic settlement + pool bootstrap) reusing the deployed CTF/Wrapped1155/UniV3 shared infra and Reality.eth v3, rather than reusing FAO registry/factory.
risks: The .env.codex PRIVATE_KEY is the live FAO Sepolia operator key (0x693E...b43d) — reusing it for ceo.futarchy.fi couples the two products' blast radius; FAO wiki documents a whole incident-response procedure for its compromise. Consider a fresh deployer key for the CEO product.

fao-testnet.summary: FAO testnet is a fully working, 100% on-chain futarchy stack on Sepolia (chainId 11155111, public RPC ethereum-sepolia.publicnode.com) with two distinct surfaces. Surface 1, the FAO v0 app (branch arbitration/onchain-futarchy-v0, local clone /home/kelvin/repos/futarchy-fi/FAO, live at testnet.fao.futarchy.ai / fao-testnet.pages.dev): a permissionless FutarchyRegistry meta-factory (0x18D1f4e5..., verified live with 1 instance) spins up per-org instances (ERC20 token + WETH bond-escalation arbitration + TWAP resolver + proposal factory + orchestrator + UniV3 spot pool); proposals graduate via bond escalation, get atomically promoted into paired YES/NO conditional UniV3 pools (CTF + Wrapped1155), and resolve by comparing YES vs NO pool TWAPs written back to Gnosis ConditionalTokens — full lifecycle already proven on-chain in May 2026. Surface 2, testnet.futarchy.ai: a separately governed genesis site (fao-governed-site repo on Cloudflare Pages) meant to be replaced by on-chain-accepted releases through the fail-closed fao-site-publisher pipeline watching SiteReleaseSelected events; its SnapshotX-based chain stack is still pre-deployment. For fire-the-CEO, the conditional-pair market machinery (CTF condition → wrapped YES/NO ERC20s → two UniV3 pools → TWAP read) and the static rankings-table UI are directly reusable; the TWAP-decides-and-settles resolver must be swapped for a real-world "did the CEO leave by T" settlement oracle.
fao-testnet.reuse_assessment: HIGHLY REUSABLE for ceo.futarchy.fi on Sepolia: (1) The conditional-pair market core — FAOOfficialProposalOrchestrator's atomic promote creating a CTF condition, Wrapped1155 ERC20 YES/NO outcome tokens, and two UniV3 pools (value-conditional-on-YES vs value-conditional-on-NO) anchored to a spot pool — is exactly the fire/keep pair structure; the 'fire premium' is simply YES-pool price minus NO-pool price, already computable from the same TWAP reads FAOTwapResolver does. All external dependencies (Seer CTF 0x8bdC504d..., Wrapped1155Factory 0xD194319D..., canonical UniV3 0x0227628f... + NPM/quoter/router) are already deployed and battle-tested on Sepolia. (2) The FutarchyRegistry meta-factory pattern (one instance per company: synthetic company token + spot pool + arbitration + resolver + factory + orchestrator, two-tx creation under the 16.7M public-RPC gas cap) maps one-to-one onto 'one futarchy per public company', including per-instance parameters (timeout, TWAP window, baseX bond). (3) The site-testnet UI is a static, no-build, indexer-free frontend (deployments.json single source of truth, EIP-6963 wallet, registry.allInstances() direct RPC reads) whose home-page 'Active futarchies' sortable rankings table (home.js) is the skeleton of the company-ranking page; its Cloudflare Pages deploy recipe (site-testnet/README.md) is the deployment recipe for ceo.futarchy.fi. NOT REUSABLE / NEEDS REPLACEMENT: (a) Resolution semantics — FAO's resolver settles the CTF condition from the TWAP comparison itself (futarchy decides AND settles); fire-the-CEO conditional markets must settle on the real-world fact 'CEO left by horizon T', so FAOTwapResolver's decision rule must be replaced by a manual/Reality.eth-style reporter while keeping its CTF-reporting plumbing; (b) company value proxy — instances mint synthetic GenericFutarchyTokens, fine as testnet stand-ins for company value but not tied to real equity; (c) the FAOSale/InsiderVesting/ragequit token-sale economics and the entire governed-site/SnapshotX/fao-site-publisher pipeline (site-release governance) are orthogonal to fire-the-CEO and should be ignored; (d) the GitHub main branch is the wrong branch — the reusable registry/app code lives only on arbitration/onchain-futarchy-v0.
risks: Branch confusion: GitHub futarchy-fi/FAO main does NOT contain the registry/site-testnet app (it holds the newer SnapshotX site-release stack + old Gnosis sale contracts); building on main would miss all reusable code, which lives on branch arbitration/onchain-futarchy-v0 (local clone /home/kelvin/repos/futarchy-fi/FAO).

Known bytecode drift: as of 2026-05-22 the live futarchy_stack_deployer on Sepolia is pre-ADAPTER_REPLACEABLE bytecode vs newer local source (coupling FFI gate fails); redeploying from current source or pinning the old commit is required before reusing the deployers as-is.

Resolution-rule mismatch is a design landmine: FAO's TWAP resolver decides ties/no-trade windows as NO via strict '>' comparison — with thin testnet liquidity a fire-the-CEO ranking computed from these pools would be noise unless each pair is seeded with meaningful liquidity and/or the settlement rule is redesigned around a real-world CEO-departure oracle.

Ops/custody: deployments.json 'active.timelock' is null and everything is operated by EOA 0x693E3FB4...; documented incident plan (setAdapter(0x0), pause auto_promote.sh, redeploy registry as v6) implies operator-key compromise is a live accepted risk on this stack.

Sepolia public-RPC gas cap (16,777,216 via publicnode) breaks single-tx instance deploys (~18.8M gas); any per-company market creation flow must keep the two-tx Part1/Part2 split or use a private RPC.

Namespace collision risk: testnet.futarchy.ai (governed genesis page, pre-deployment chain stack) vs testnet.fao.futarchy.ai (actual FAO app) are different products; deployments.json is manual ('generated_by: manual') and the wiki notes address-drift gaps, so verify any address on-chain before reuse (registry and arbitration were re-verified live on 2026-07-16 in this sweep).

The fao-site-publisher/governed-site pipeline is operationally heavy (dual-RPC witnesses, HMAC state repo, deploy-key rulesets) and irrelevant to fire-the-CEO; adopting it would add cost without value — plain Cloudflare Pages publishing (as site-testnet does) suffices for ceo.futarchy.fi.

data-layer.summary: futarchy-api (repo name "futarchy-charts") is a small Express server (~2.8k LOC core) serving per-proposal chart data: a unified v2 chart endpoint, a legacy v1 prices endpoint, spot candles, and GraphQL passthroughs. It reads from two Checkpoint-based indexers (registry + candles GraphQL), GeckoTerminal (or a futarchy-spot SQLite service) for spot prices, and direct RPC for ERC-4626 rates and a SnapshotLinkRegistry lookup; chains are hardcoded to Gnosis (100) and Ethereum (1), with zero Sepolia references in either repo. futarchy-indexers is @snapshot-labs/checkpoint-based (Docker + Postgres), with a multichain candles indexer (Gnosis Algebra + Mainnet UniswapV3, OHLCV candles/swaps/pools) and a Gnosis-only registry indexer, plus ~8 monkey-patch scripts on the Checkpoint framework and a reorg/wedge watchdog. Adding a Sepolia chain to the candles indexer is a documented config-level extension, but only if the new contracts emit the exact FutarchyFactory NewProposal + UniswapV3/Algebra pool event signatures; new purpose-built contracts would also require writers.ts changes, and the whole stack is proposal-centric with no ranking/list endpoint anyway. Verdict: do NOT reuse this stack for ceo.futarchy.fi — direct RPC reads (one view function returning all companies' conditional prices) plus a tiny cron appending to a JSON snapshot for sparklines and the ranking table is strictly less work, less ops, and covers all three requirements.

data-layer.reuse_assessment: Recommendation: do NOT reuse the indexer+API stack; build direct-RPC reads plus a tiny cron-built JSON snapshot. Rationale: (1) The three product needs are cheap without indexing — (a) current conditional prices: since we control the new Sepolia contracts, expose one view function (e.g. getAllCompanies() returning company, yesPool price, noPool price, horizon) and read it client-side or server-side via ethers in one eth_call; futarchy-api itself already does direct-RPC reads for its registry lookups, so this is an established pattern in-house. (b) Sparklines: a cron (systemd timer / GitHub Action, every 5-15 min) calling that same view function and appending {timestamp, prices[]} to a JSON file gives adequate sparkline resolution with ~100-200 lines total; swap-level OHLCV via Checkpoint is massive overkill for sparklines. (c) Ranking table: computed in the same cron pass (fire premium = P(value|leave) - P(value|stay)) and served as a static JSON — no endpoint exists for this in futarchy-api anyway, so reuse saves nothing. (2) Reuse costs are real: the candles indexer needs a new sepoliaConfig AND writers.ts changes unless the new contracts replicate the exact FutarchyFactory NewProposal + UniswapV3/Algebra event signatures and token-role scheme; the registry indexer is single-chain Gnosis-only; the stack requires Docker+Postgres+patched Checkpoint+watchdog on a VM with a documented history of reorg wedges; resetOnConfigChange:true risks full resyncs; spot pricing (GeckoTerminal) is dead on Sepolia. What IS reusable: the /api/v2/proposals/:id/chart response shape (if we want to reuse an existing frontend chart component), the rate-provider and tiered-cache utility patterns as reference code, the Algebra/UniV3 pool-price math in writers.ts (sqrtPrice→price with isInverted handling) if the new markets use UniV3-style pools on Sepolia, and — only if deep candle history becomes a requirement later AND we deploy the existing FutarchyFactory bytecode to Sepolia — the documented addIndexer('sepolia', ...) extension path in proposals-candles/checkpoint/src/index.ts. Backfill note: if sparkline history before cron-start is ever needed, replay Swap events via eth_getLogs directly rather than standing up Checkpoint.
risks: Cron-snapshot approach: price history only exists from the moment the cron starts — deploy the cron at market creation time, or accept a one-off eth_getLogs backfill script if history is needed retroactively

Cron is a single point of staleness: a dead timer silently serves old prices/ranking — embed generatedAt in the JSON, render it in the UI, and add a status-bot check (existing @azhermes pattern monitors bond.futarchy.ai the same way)

Public Sepolia RPC rate limits/flakiness: a per-page-load multicall for N companies is fine, but use a keyed RPC (Infura/Alchemy Sepolia) for the cron; both repos document free-RPC getLogs limits as a recurring pain point

If the team later reverses course and reuses the Checkpoint stack: resetOnConfigChange:true can force a full resync when adding a chain; the 8 framework monkey-patches pin an old @snapshot-labs/checkpoint version; the watchdog exists because of real production reorg wedges on the futarchy-indexers VM

Designing the view function poorly (per-company calls instead of one batch getter) recreates the N-request problem the warmer/cache machinery in futarchy-api was built to paper over — keep the one-call-returns-everything contract design

Thin testnet AMM liquidity means the fire premium ranking is trivially manipulable by a single swap; the data layer will faithfully report it — the product/design doc should decide how to display or dampen this (e.g. TWAP in the view function rather than spot tick price)

GeckoTerminal-based spot overlay and currency_stable_rate conversion from the existing stack have no Sepolia equivalents — do not carry those response fields into the new API shape expecting them to populate
